user.title: OVH SMS — API Credentials
user.description:
Enter your OVH API credentials.

**How to get your keys:** go to [eu.api.ovh.com/createToken]({create_token_url}) and create a token with:
- GET `/me`
- GET `/sms` and `/sms/*`
- POST `/sms/*/jobs`

Set **Validity** to **Unlimited**.

Your **Service Name** is found in [OVH Manager]({ovh_manager_url}) → Telecom → SMS (format: `sms-xx12345-1`).

data_description.application_key: Also called AK — found on the token creation confirmation page
data_description.application_secret: Also called AS — keep this secret!
data_description.consumer_key: Also called CK — authorizes this app to act on your behalf
data_description.recipients: Phone numbers to send SMS to, separated by commas. E.g. `+33612345678, +33698765432`
data_description.sender: Alphanumeric name shown as SMS sender (max 11 chars). Leave empty to use an auto-assigned short number

validation_failed.title: OVH SMS — Validation Failed
validation_failed.description:
Could not validate your configuration:

**{error_detail}**

This can happen if your keys are wrong, the service name doesn't exist, or OVH API is temporarily unreachable.

data.action: What would you like to do?

rate_limit.title: OVH SMS — Rate Limiting
rate_limit.description:
Protect against notification bursts to avoid draining your SMS credits.

If your Home Assistant triggers many automations at once (e.g. all motion sensors fire), rate limiting prevents sending hundreds of SMS in seconds.

data_description.rate_limit_strategy:
**drop** — excess messages silently discarded (best for repetitive alerts)
**queue** — excess messages wait and send later (best for critical alerts)
**disabled** — no throttling at all
data_description.rate_limit_max: How many SMS can be sent within the time window (1–1000). Ignored when disabled
data_description.rate_limit_window: Sliding window in seconds. Example: 10 SMS per 60s = max 10 SMS/minute (10–86400). Ignored when disabled
data_description.rate_limit_queue_size: Max messages waiting in line before oldest are dropped (1–500). Only used with **queue** strategy

error.cannot_connect: Cannot reach OVH API. Check your internet connection.
error.invalid_auth: Invalid credentials. Double-check your Application Key, Secret and Consumer Key.
error.service_not_found: SMS service not found. Verify the service name in your OVH Manager.
error.invalid_recipients: Invalid phone numbers — use E.164 format: +33612345678
error.unknown: An unexpected error occurred. Check Home Assistant logs.

abort.already_configured: This SMS service is already configured.
abort.import_failed: Failed to import YAML configuration. Check logs for details.

test_sms.title: OVH SMS — Send a test SMS
test_sms.description: Send a test SMS to your configured recipients: **{recipients}**

rate_limit.title: OVH SMS — Rate Limiting
rate_limit.description: Adjust rate limiting. Changes take effect immediately (no restart needed).

data_description.rate_limit_strategy: **drop** — discard excess · **queue** — delay excess · **disabled** — no limit
data_description.rate_limit_queue_size: Max queued messages (1–500, queue strategy only)

help.title: OVH SMS — How to use
help.description:
## Your notify entity

**`{entity_id}`**

---

## Trigger from an automation

In the automation editor: *Actions* → *Call service* → *Notify: Send message* → select **`{entity_id}`**.

Or in YAML:

```yaml
service: notify.send_message
target:
  entity_id: {entity_id}
data:
  message: "Motion detected at front door!"
```

---

## Advanced message parameters

| Parameter | Values | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `sender` | text (max 11 chars) | Override the default sender |
| `priority` | `high` `medium` `low` `veryLow` | Message priority |
| `coding` | `7bit` `unicode` | `7bit` = 160 chars · `unicode` = accents, 70 chars |
| `no_stop_clause` | `true` / `false` | `false` adds a STOP clause (default: `true`) |

**SMS service:** `{service_name}`

error.no_recipients: No recipients configured. Go to API credentials to add phone numbers.
error.test_failed: SMS send failed. Check your credentials and OVH account.
